expect: Test severity (Popper demarcation) predicts experiment quality with r>=0.4. High-severity experiments (>=0.5) have 3x+ higher quality scores. Severity trend improving since L-1560 (S531). FM-38 rate correlates with low severity.
actual: r(severity, quality) = +0.603 (n=475, strong). High-severity mean quality 0.452 vs low-severity 0.239 (1.9x ratio, not 3x). Severity trend DECLINING: S>=520 mean=0.291 vs S<520 mean=0.318 despite L-1560 awareness. 86% of experiments have no check_mode field. Full corpus: mean quality 36.6%, significance 10%, pre-registration 39%, falsification lanes 95/1600 (5.9% vs 20% target).
diff: Predicted r>=0.4: got +0.603 (CONFIRMED, stronger than expected). Predicted 3x ratio: got 1.9x (PARTIALLY CONFIRMED, direction correct). Predicted severity improving: FALSIFIED — severity is declining in recent sessions. Root cause: L-1560 diagnosed the gap but created no structural enforcement (L-601 pattern). check_mode field absent in 86% means mode enforcement is voluntary → decays.
prescriptions: P-SQ1: open_lane.py should require check_mode in experiment JSON (structural enforcement, not advisory)
P-SQ2: science_quality.py should flag experiments with severity < 0.3 as WEAK (visibility pressure)
P-SQ3: target significance criterion improvement from 10% to 25% by S560 (add effect size template to experiment JSON schema)
lessons_learned: L-1560 diagnosed Popper demarcation gap but created no enforcement → severity declined (L-601 pattern)
Severity-quality correlation r=0.603 is the strongest single predictor of experiment quality in the corpus
86% missing check_mode means mode-based routing (falsification lanes) has no experiment-level anchor
Recent quality improvement (43% vs 36.6% full corpus) comes from better controls, not better severity
falsification_conditions: Falsified if r(severity, quality) < 0.3 on independent sample, or if severity enforcement produces no quality improvement within 20 sessions
